source_paper_title: Improved Bounds for Single-Nomination Impartial Selection
context:
Page 14, Section 5 (Upper Bounds), last paragraph before Theorem 5.1; see also Page 3, Section 1.1 (Our Contribution), last sentence

Like the bounds of Fischer and Klimm they approach 3/4 as n goes to infinity, and thus do not rule out the existence of an impartial mechanism that is close to 3/4-optimal when n is large. Whether such a mechanism exists is an intriguing open question, the resolution of which is likely to require new insights.
title: Existence of an impartial selection mechanism achieving approximation ratio approaching \(3/4\) in the single-nomination model
latex:
Given an integer \(n\ge 2\), let \(G_n\) be the set of directed graphs \(G=(V,E)\) with vertex set \(V=\{1,2,\dots,n\}\), no self-loops, and outdegree exactly \(1\) at every vertex, i.e., for every \(v\in V\) there exists exactly one \(u\in V\setminus\{v\}\) with \((v,u)\in E\). For \(v\in V\), let \(\delta^-(v,G)=|\{u\in V:(u,v)\in E\}|\) be the indegree of \(v\), and let \(\Delta(G)=\max_{v\in V}\delta^-(v,G)\) be the maximum indegree.

A (randomized) selection mechanism is a family \(f=\{f^{(n)}\}_{n\ge 2}\) where each \(f^{(n)}\) maps every \(G\in G_n\) to a probability distribution \(f^{(n)}(G)\in[0,1]^n\) over \(V\) satisfying \(\sum_{v\in V} f^{(n)}_v(G)=1\). The mechanism is impartial if for every \(n\ge 2\), every \(G=(V,E)\in G_n\), every \(v\in V\), and every alternative edge set \(E'\) with \(E\setminus(\{v\}\times V)=E'\setminus(\{v\}\times V)\), it holds that \(f^{(n)}_v(V,E)=f^{(n)}_v(V,E')\); i.e., the selection probability of \(v\) does not depend on \(v\)'s outgoing edge.

For \(\alpha\in[0,1]\), we say \(f\) is \(\alpha\)-optimal on \(G_n\) if for every \(G\in G_n\) with \(\Delta(G)>0\),
\[
\mathbb{E}_{v\sim f^{(n)}(G)}\big[\delta^-(v,G)\big] \ge \alpha\,\Delta(G).
\]

Problem: Determine whether the asymptotically optimal approximation ratio equals \(3/4\). Concretely, is the following statement true?

For every \(\varepsilon>0\) there exists an integer \(N\) and an impartial selection mechanism \(f=\{f^{(n)}\}_{n\ge 2}\) such that for all \(n\ge N\), the mechanism \(f^{(n)}\) is \((3/4-\varepsilon)\)-optimal on \(G_n\), i.e., for every \(G\in G_n\) with \(\Delta(G)>0\),
\[
\mathbb{E}_{v\sim f^{(n)}(G)}\big[\delta^-(v,G)\big] \ge (3/4-\varepsilon)\,\Delta(G).
\]
Equivalently, does there exist a sequence of impartial mechanisms whose worst-case optimality factors \(\alpha_n\) satisfy \(\liminf_{n\to\infty}\alpha_n \ge 3/4\)?
